interpretation: Cross-root metrics are implemented and evaluated as diagnostics.  They should not be treated as selectors unless they survive exact-control held-out tests.  In the current data, cross-root co-participation improves some small-sample fits after load-stratum control, but it does not rescue the prospective high/low causal contrast by itself.  In the full-coupling normal-fan experiment, binary cross-root overlap saturates at one and only controllability/rank-style cross-root summaries vary.
